seoTitle: How to Add a Link to an Image in Webflow (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to turn a static image into a clickable hyperlink in Webflow using Link Block containers, without breaking your responsive layout.
intro: Turning a plain image into a clickable link is one of the most common tasks in Webflow, whether you're linking a banner to a landing page or building a visual portfolio grid. This guide walks through wrapping an image inside a Link Block so it stays interactive without breaking your site's structure. You'll come away knowing how to set link destinations, hover states, and alt text for SEO.

steps.heading: Open the Add panel and find the Link Block
steps.body: In the Webflow Designer, open the Add Elements panel and locate the Link Block component under the basic elements section.

steps.heading: Drag the Link Block onto the canvas
steps.body: Place the Link Block where you want the clickable image to appear, then nest an image element inside it so the whole block becomes clickable.

steps.heading: Set the link destination
steps.body: Open the element settings panel and paste in the destination URL, or choose an internal page or anchor link instead.

steps.heading: Choose a target and hover behavior
steps.body: Decide whether the link should open in the same tab or a new one, and style any hover state so users get visual feedback.

steps.heading: Add alt text and publish
steps.body: Give the image descriptive alt text for accessibility and SEO, then publish your project and click through to confirm the link works.

faq.question: Can I link an image without using a Link Block?
faq.answer: Webflow's cleanest approach is nesting the image inside a Link Block, since it keeps the markup semantic and avoids custom code.

faq.question: Will linking an image affect my page's SEO?
faq.answer: Not negatively, as long as you keep meaningful alt text on the image and use a real anchor element, which Link Blocks do by default.

faq.question: Can I link to a different page within my own site?
faq.answer: Yes, the link settings panel lets you pick an internal page or a section anchor instead of typing a full URL.

faq.question: Does the linked image work the same on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, since the Link Block is a standard responsive element, but it's worth testing tap targets on smaller breakpoints to ensure the image is easy to tap.
